title: E2E Data Flow Map for Security-Sensitive Journeys
description: Consolidated front-to-back flows, validations, states, and audit points across Call Center and BaNCS Back Office.
testDescription:
Journey 1: Agent login / SSO / MFA / lockout (ASSUMPTION for MFA)
Input in Call Center -> Username/password in SSO screen (Doc A: 2.1)
Request to Backend -> SSO token validation; role fetch; login attempt counter (ASSUMPTION)
Backend validations -> Account status active; failed-attempt threshold; RBAC scope (Doc A: 13, 12.5; Doc B: validations generally)
Backend state change -> Session created; attempt counters updated; lockout flag on threshold
Response to Call Center -> Login success/failure; masked error
Verify -> UI shows Dashboard (Doc A: 2.2); Backend audit: LOGIN_SUCCESS/FAIL with user, IP, terminal, timestamp.

Journey 2: Customer identification + verification (ASSUMPTION: optional OTP/KBA)
Call Center -> Service Mode + Identification Type/Number OR Portfolio Ref (Doc A: 2.1)
Backend -> Lookup BP ID/Portfolio; KBA/OTP validation (ASSUMPTION)
Backend state -> Customer context bound to session; verification result recorded
Response -> Portfolio List; masking of references (Doc A: 2.1/2.2)
Verify -> UI shows portfolio list; Backend audit: CUSTOMER_VERIFY with BP ID, portfolio refs (masked in UI).

Journey 3: Sensitive profile change (ASSUMPTION: if present; not covered in docs)
Call Center -> Edit email/phone; step-up auth
Backend -> RBAC check; write change; PII masking in logs
State -> Contact updated; audit trail
Response -> Success banner without showing full PII
Verify -> Audit: PROFILE_UPDATE with before/after (masked) + approver if any.

Journey 4: Equity/ETF/MF order placement end-to-end
Call Center -> Create -> Preview -> Confirm (Doc A: 3.2, 3.3, 3.4)
Backend -> Pre-trade checks: funds/positions/product/limits; TASE self-transaction rule (Doc A: 17.1; Doc B: 2 Validations)
State -> Order Authorized -> Placed With Market -> Executed/Partial -> Settled (Doc B: 3.1/3.2 lifecycle)
Response -> Order/Trade Book updates; commission preview (Doc A: 3.2)
Verify -> UI Order/Trade Book; Backend audit: ORDER_CREATE/MODIFY/CANCEL/EXECUTE; files 1051/1052 recon; fee postings.

Journey 5: Security Transfer Within/Outside Bank/Virtual Sell
Call Center -> Select Security -> Add Beneficiary -> Preview -> Release -> Authorization (Doc A: 12.x)
Backend -> Validate MU match; same-entity; ex-date prohibition; TASE-only for Outside Bank; virtual sell target=source (Doc A: 12.1/12.2/12.5; Doc B: 4.1–4.1.3)
State -> Released -> Authorized (checker(s)) -> Pend Settle/Open Delivery -> Settled (Doc B: 4 flows + 1051/1052)
Response -> Confirmation; Authorization Queue; Transfer History (Doc A: 12.4–12.5)
Verify -> UI history; Backend: open delivery records; file 15/132 out/in; 1051/1052 matching; tax-lot carryover.

Journey 6: Tax Simulation -> Sell -> Settlement/Tax
Call Center -> Tax Simulation select -> Simulate -> Sell (Doc A: 4.2)
Backend -> Call MATRIX via BO; compute tax; on sell settlement, post tax/refund (Doc B: 6)
State -> Lots closed; tax postings
Response -> Simulation results then order path -> confirmation
Verify -> UI shows tax preview; Backend movements + tax postings; audit TAX_SIMULATE and SELL_ORDER.

Journey 7: MF price correction (reverse/reallocate)
Call Center -> MF order placed; later price correction displayed in history (Doc A: 3.3, 3.5.2)
Backend -> Auto/Manual reversal & reallocation via 1051/1052; settlement reversal/new price (Doc B: 3.6)
State -> Allocation reversed; reallocated; cash/units adjusted
Response -> Order Trail shows reversal/reallocation
Verify -> Backend movements; audit PRICE_CORRECTION; fees re-applied appropriately.

Journey 8: Reconciliation & Net Settlement
Call Center -> Order/Trade history views; status and warnings (Doc A: 3.5–3.8, 9)
Backend -> Process 1051, 1052, 1054; 32/1091; 871/872; update recon statuses (Doc B: 3.1, 3.2, 8)
State -> Reconciled/Not Matched/Failed; settlement advice linked
Response -> Reports; exceptions list
Verify -> Files processed; statuses updated; exception reports generated.

Journey 9: Call recording access control (ASSUMPTION)
Call Center -> Search/play/export call recording requires RBAC and audit
Backend -> Authorize; stream token; watermark; audit RECORDING_ACCESS
State -> Access logs
Response -> Player opens with masked PII metadata
Verify -> Audit events; no raw PII in URLs.

Terminology Mapping (ASSUMPTION where explicit mapping not labeled)
- Customer Reference/Portfolio Reference (Call Center) = Portfolio Ref (Backend) [Doc A: 2.1; Doc B: 3.x]
- Internal Order Number (Call Center) = Order Id/Deal/Allocation Id (Backend) [Doc A: 3.5/3.6; Doc B: 3.1]
- BP ID (Backend) = Customer BP Identifier (Call Center) [Doc A: 2.1; Doc B: 4]
- Open Delivery (Backend) = Pend Settle (Call Center status post-authorization) [Doc A: 12.5; Doc B: 3.1/4]
- Settlement Advice (Backend) = File 1052 confirmation elements [Doc B: 3.1]
- Same Entity (Call Center) = Same entity tax identifier (Backend) [Doc A: 12.2 table; Doc B: 4]
- Authorization Queue/My Queue (Call Center) = Checker1/Checker2 workflow state (Backend) [Doc A: 12.5, 13]
- Virtual Sell (Call Center) = Within-bank transfer with source=target portfolio (Backend) [Doc A: 12.2; Doc B: 4.1.1]
ASSUMPTION: Audit event names are indicative; actual table/topic names to be confirmed.
prerequisites: Access to Call Center (FO) and BaNCS Back Office (BO) lower environment; test user roles: Agent, Supervisor/Checker, Auditor; seeded portfolios with local/foreign holdings; file drop simulators for TASE 1051/1052/1054/32/1091 and foreign 871/872; Kafka/log access (ASSUMPTION).
stepsToPerform: This is a reference map, not an executable case.
expectedResult: Stakeholders can trace security-sensitive journeys end-to-end with clear validation points, state transitions, and audit fields for testing.

title: SSO Login, MFA, and Account Lockout with Audit
description: Verify secure SSO login, MFA step-up (ASSUMPTION), failed-attempt lockout, masked errors, and audit logging.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 2.1 Logging on; 2.2 Dashboard; 13 Authorization (limits, queues). ASSUMPTION: MFA via OTP for step-up; lockout threshold=5 attempts. Systems: Both (Call Center initiates; Backend authorizes/logs). Roles: Agent, Auditor.
Audit & Logs: Expect LOGIN_ATTEMPT, LOGIN_SUCCESS/FAIL, MFA_CHALLENGE/VERIFY, ACCOUNT_LOCKED with fields userId, role, branch/MU, IP, terminal id, timestamp; no password/OTP in logs.
Security Assertions: - No sensitive info in error messages; - OTP one-time and expires; - Lockout enforced; - Session token regenerated post-MFA; - PII masked on header widgets.
Automation: High (Playwright/Selenium + API + log parsing).
prerequisites: Agent user exists and active; wrong password known; MFA delivery simulator enabled (ASSUMPTION).
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification (DB/Log/API) |
| 1 | Call Center | Navigate to login screen and enter valid username with wrong password 5 times | Error shows 'Invalid credentials' without specifics; account locked on 5th try (ASSUMPTION) | BO auth logs show 5 LOGIN_FAIL; ACCOUNT_LOCKED flag true for user |
| 2 | Call Center | Try login again with correct password | Error: 'Account locked. Contact admin' | Audit LOGIN_BLOCKED; no session created |
| 3 | Backend | Admin unlocks account (out of scope UI) | Account status active | Audit USER_UNLOCK with admin id |
| 4 | Call Center | Login with valid credentials | MFA prompt displayed (ASSUMPTION) | Audit MFA_CHALLENGE issued; OTP ID generated |
| 5 | Call Center | Enter wrong OTP once | Error 'Invalid code' without code length hints | Audit MFA_VERIFY_FAIL; OTP remains single-use |
| 6 | Call Center | Enter correct OTP | Login success; Dashboard loads (Doc A: 2.2) | Audit LOGIN_SUCCESS; session token created; role fetched |
| 7 | Call Center | Verify widgets render with any PII masked | PII masked in UI (e.g., account XXX-XX1456) | UI snapshot; server-side response excludes full PAN/ID |
| 8 | Backend | Check active session and expiry | Session TTL per policy; idle timeout configured | Session store shows expiry timestamps; no secrets in logs |
| 9 | Call Center | Logout | Redirect to login; session invalidated | Audit LOGOUT; session token revoked |
| 10 | Call Center | Back-button or reuse old URL | Redirect to login; no session restoration | No active session in store; 401 on API calls
expectedResult: Secure SSO login with MFA; account lockout after failed attempts; masked error messages; proper session creation/termination; complete audit trail.
expectedResult_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Attempt to brute-force OTP beyond 3 tries -> temporary step-up lock; 2) Try session fixation by reusing pre-login JSESSIONID -> new session ID generated post-auth.

title: Customer Identification, KBA/OTP, and Anti-Enumeration
description: Validate customer lookup by National ID/Portfolio with anti-enumeration, optional OTP, and PII masking.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 2.1 Customer Authentication; Figures 2–3. ASSUMPTION: Step-up OTP on risky channel (Phone). Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Supervisor (for override if any).
Audit: CUSTOMER_SEARCH, CUSTOMER_VERIFY_SUCCESS/FAIL with BP ID (masked in UI), portfolio refs; IP and terminal.
Security Assertions: - No indication whether ID exists; - OTP single-use; - Masked portfolio in UI.
prerequisites: Customer BP ID exists with two portfolios; phone +972*****1234; OTP service simulator.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Open Customer Authentication; select Service Mode=Phone; Identification Type=National Id; enter '0*******664' | Neutral prompt, no existence hint | No CUSTOMER_FOUND log until submit |
| 2 | Call Center | Click Authenticate | If risk policy triggers, OTP challenge displayed (ASSUMPTION) | BO logs CUSTOMER_SEARCH with id hash; MFA_CHALLENGE sent |
| 3 | Call Center | Enter correct OTP | Portfolio List displays; portfolio refs masked e.g., 04-131-2****7 | Audit CUSTOMER_VERIFY_SUCCESS with BP ID; UI masking verified |
| 4 | Call Center | Select a portfolio and Submit | Trading tiles enabled for that portfolio | Session context bound to portfolio; audit CONTEXT_SET |
| 5 | Call Center | Try wrong National ID (random) 3 times | Generic error 'Cannot authenticate' with same latency | BO logs verify fails without disclosing presence/absence |
| 6 | Backend | Verify logs do not contain full ID | Only hashed/partial ID | Log scan/PII lint |
| 7 | Call Center | Try Portfolio Reference path with valid partially masked entry | Success list for portfolios linked | Audit includes portfolioRef; UI masked |
| 8 | Call Center | Attempt to paste full PAN 4111********1111 in any input | Field rejects PAN; or stored without exposure | No PAN persisted in logs
expectedResult: Agent can authenticate customer with step-up OTP and receive masked portfolio list; anti-enumeration enforced; PII not leaked in UI/errors/logs.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Replay OTP within window -> rejected as already used; 2) Timing attack—ensure constant error timing for existing vs non-existing IDs (ASSUMPTION: within ±100ms).

title: Market Data Entitlements and Masking (Live vs Delayed)
description: Ensure market data entitlements per Table 3 are enforced across widgets and order panels, with proper delayed banners and no leakage.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 11 Market Data Subscription Table 3; 6 Market View; 3.2/3.3 order panels. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent with local-live entitlement; foreign-delayed only.
Security Assertions: - Delayed data tagged; - Order Depth live only per entitlement; - No bypass via hidden endpoints; - UI banner 'Prices are delayed...' shown.
Audit: ENTITLEMENT_DECISION events when fetching Quote/Depth with fields: user, exchange, feature, entitlement outcome.
prerequisites: User entitlements configured: Local=Live, Foreign=Delayed. Portfolio with both local (TASE) and foreign holdings.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Open Market Watch for TASE symbols | Live quotes and Order Depth available | ENTITLEMENT_DECISION=ALLOW for local/live |
| 2 | Call Center | Open Get Quote for foreign symbol | Delayed data with banner 'Prices are delayed for Foreign' (Doc A: widgets note) | ENTITLEMENT_DECISION=DELAYED; timestamp offset >=15m (configurable) |
| 3 | Call Center | From order entry, click Order Depth for TASE | Depth opens live | API call includes entitlement token validated in BO logs |
| 4 | Call Center | Attempt Order Depth for foreign | Denied or delayed per Table 3 | ENTITLEMENT_DECISION=DENY or DELAY per setup |
| 5 | Call Center | ETF List and choose Buy (equity path) | Live quote on equity path | Audit shows feature=ETF_equity_path |
| 6 | Call Center | Open Portfolio Valuation | Based on customer privilege; delayed if configured | ENTITLEMENT_DECISION recorded |
| 7 | Backend | Check no foreign live feed served | No live payloads for foreign to this user | Log inspect for feed fanout |
| 8 | Call Center | Export list to Excel | Export contains no PII beyond permitted; prices reflect entitlement | Export service logs sanitized
expectedResult: Entitlements strictly enforced: local live, foreign delayed; appropriate UI indicators and no unauthorized live depth/quotes.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Manually hit foreign depth REST endpoint -> 403; 2) Change client clock to bypass delay -> server-side delay still enforced.

title: TASE Self-Transaction Block at BP Level
description: Block opposite order that would cross self per TASE rules; verify BP-level scope and secure error.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 17.1 TASE Self-Transaction; 3.2 Order placement; Doc B: 2 Validations. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent.
Security Assertions: - Rule enforced at BP level, not portfolio; - Error message per spec without extra data; - Logged decision with price comparison logic.
Audit: SELF_TXN_CHECK with fields: BP ID, symbol, side, incoming price, open order id/price, phase=Continuous.
prerequisites: BP has an open pending Sell order on symbol S at price 100; Agent tries to place Buy in same BP via different portfolio.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Authenticate customer; select different portfolio under same BP | Context set | Audit CONTEXT_SET with BP ID |
| 2 | Call Center | Place Buy MKT for symbol S during Continuous phase | System blocks per rule a) | Error 'Self-Transactions are not allowed in TASE' |
| 3 | Backend | Evaluate open orders for BP, match opposite and compare | Detect opposite Sell 100; MKT would cross | SELF_TXN_CHECK logged with decision=BLOCK |
| 4 | Call Center | Try Buy limit 101 | Blocked (>= open Sell 100) | Same audit |
| 5 | Call Center | Try Buy limit 99 | Allowed to proceed validations | SELF_TXN_CHECK decision=ALLOW |
| 6 | Backend | Continue pre-trade checks and authorize | Order status Authorized | ORDER_CREATE audit |
| 7 | Call Center | Attempt similar with two portfolios | Still blocked at BP scope | Audit shows portfolio ids but BP same |
| 8 | Backend | Ensure market phase considered | If not Continuous, behavior per rules (ASSUMPTION) | Phase logged in decision
expectedResult: Opposite self-crossing orders at BP level are blocked with correct price comparisons; allowed when not crossing; clear audit trail.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Attempt to split across portfolios to bypass block -> still blocked; 2) Modify existing order to cause self-cross -> modify rejected.

title: Equity Buy with Maker–Checker (Limit Exceeded) and Audit
description: Place equity buy order triggering 'Maker Limit Exceeded' requiring supervisor approval; verify queues, approval, execution, and audit.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 3.2 Create/Preview/Confirm; 13 Authorization; Figures 76–79; Order Alerts; Doc B: 2 Validations; 3.1 Trade cycle. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent (Maker), Supervisor (Checker1), possibly Checker2.
Security Assertions: - Maker cannot self-approve; - Assignment to specific vs pool; - Full audit of approval chain; - Secure error if rejected.
Audit: ORDER_CREATE; EXCEPTION_RAISED code 610070; AUTH_ASSIGN; AUTH_DECISION; ORDER_PLACED; 1051/1052 recon entries.
prerequisites: Agent limit set below order amount; sufficient funds; symbol LPSN.D (example).
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Create Buy order amount exceeding maker limit; click Place Order | Preview shows commission; proceed | ORDER_CREATE pending auth; EXCEPTION_RAISED 610070 |
| 2 | Call Center | On approval popup, choose Assign to Specific authorizer 'sup01' with remarks | Request assigned | AUTH_ASSIGN with maker, checker id |
| 3 | Backend | Checker opens My Queue (Doc A: 12.5/13) | Sees pending transaction | Queue item with ref, amount, portfolio |
| 4 | Backend | Checker reviews and Approves | Status U–Authorized; order routed | AUTH_DECISION=APPROVE; ORDER_AUTHORIZED |
| 5 | Call Center | Refresh Order Status | Status Pending/Executed as market responds | UI shows internal order number; trade book updates on execution |
| 6 | Backend | Ingest 1051 executions | Update deals; recon status=Reconciled | File watcher marks matched |
| 7 | Backend | Process 1052 settlement | Movements posted; blocks adjusted | Settlement advice matched; cash/security movements |
| 8 | Call Center | Verify Trade Book shows executed qty/price | Trade line present | Trade record linked to order id |
| 9 | Backend | Check E-Journal 'Initiated By Me' and 'Authorized By Me' | Entries visible to respective users | E-Journal tables updated
expectedResult: Order held for authorization due to maker limit, approved by supervisor, executed and settled with complete audit chain and reconciliations.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Maker tries to assign to self -> blocked; 2) Checker rejects -> status shows 'Approval Rejected' and no market routing.

title: ETF Order Path Selection Security (Equity vs Mutual Fund)
description: Ensure ETF order invokes correct path chooser and entitlements, commissions, and audit trail.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 3.4 ETF List and Panel Alert (Fig. 22), 3.2/3.3 flows. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent.
Security Assertions: - Path chooser modal required; - Fees/preview match path; - No hidden switch after authorization; - Audit includes path.
Audit: ETF_PATH_SELECTED with values equity_path or mutual_fund_path; ORDER_CREATE; COMMISSION_PREVIEW.
prerequisites: ETF symbol available in list; user entitlements per Table 3.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Open ETF List and click Buy | Panel alert prompts for order path | UI modal displayed (Fig. 22) |
| 2 | Call Center | Select Equity Order Path and proceed | Equity order entry opens with live quote | ETF_PATH_SELECTED logged=equity_path |
| 3 | Call Center | Fill order and preview | Commission per equity tariff; preview shows details | COMMISSION_PREVIEW logged |
| 4 | Call Center | Confirm | Internal order created | ORDER_CREATE with instrument_type=ETF; path=equity |
| 5 | Backend | Pre-trade validations | Order authorized | Logs show equity validations |
| 6 | Call Center | Repeat via Mutual Fund Order Path (Purchase) | MF purchase screen; NAV-based preview | path=mf_path; latest NAV fields present |
| 7 | Backend | Validate MF trading parameters (Doc A: 3.3/Doc B: 3.6) | Order accepted | Param checks logged |
| 8 | Backend | Process allocations/settlement per path | Equity via 1051/1052; MF via 1051/544/1052 | Reconciliations updated
expectedResult: ETF path selection enforced; appropriate screens, entitlements, and fee calculations; consistent audit of path and charges.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Try to deep-link MF path for user lacking MF entitlement -> denied; 2) Switch path after approval -> server rejects mismatch.

title: Mutual Fund Redeem with Price Correction (Reverse/Reallocate)
description: Place MF redeem, then validate backend auto price correction via reversal/reallocation and settlement adjustments.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 3.3 Purchase/Redeem, MF Order Book/Trail; Doc B: 3.6 MF trading allocation/settlement and price correction. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Back Office operator (monitor only).
Security Assertions: - Corrections produce immutable reversal and reallocation entries; - Customer-facing data updates without PII leak; - Fees consistent.
Audit: MF_ORDER_CREATE; MF_ALLOCATION; MF_PRICE_CORRECTION_AUTO (from 1051); REVERSAL_POSTED; REALLOCATION_POSTED; SETTLEMENT_REVERSED; SETTLEMENT_APPLIED.
prerequisites: Customer holds MF units; bank processes 1051 and 1052; a price correction event will be simulated in T+1/T+2.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Redeem MF via NAV List -> Redeem; enter units; Place Order | Preview shows NAV/cut-off; confirm | MF_ORDER_CREATE logged |
| 2 | Backend | End of day receive 1051 allocation | Allocation captured | Allocation record created; custody blocks handled |
| 3 | Backend | Process T+1 1052 settlement | Cash settlement posted | Settlement advice matched; movements posted |
| 4 | Backend | Receive 1051 reversal/reallocation (auto) | Old allocation reversed; new price allocated | MF_PRICE_CORRECTION_AUTO; reversal + new allocation rows |
| 5 | Backend | Process 1052 reversal/settlement | Reverse prior street cash; apply new | Movement pairs created with references |
| 6 | Call Center | View MF Order Trail | Shows reversal and reallocation entries | Trail contains timestamps and new NAV |
| 7 | Backend | Verify distribution/custody fees as applicable | Fees recalculated consistently | Fee postings present once per final state |
| 8 | Backend | Ensure tax postings updated (Doc B: 6) | Correct tax/refund adjustments | Tax engine postings updated
expectedResult: MF redemption proceeds normally; later auto price correction reverses and reallocates with updated settlement and consistent audit/fees.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Manual price correction while auto already applied -> second change prevented or sequenced; 2) Duplicate 1051 reversal ignored idempotently.

title: Security Transfer Outside Bank with Same/Different Entity and Ex-Date Block (Maker–Checker)
description: End-to-end outside-bank transfer enforcing TASE-only, same-entity logic, ex-date prohibition, maker–checker approvals, and settlement via files 15/1051/1052.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 12.1–12.5 Security Transfer (validations, preview, authorization); Table validations for ex-date, TASE-only Outside Bank; Doc B: 4.1–4.1.3 flows and 1051/1052/15/132. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent (Maker), Checker1/Checker2.
Security Assertions: - Outside Bank disabled for non-TASE; - Beneficiary bank/branch codes validated; - 'Transfer without Identification (Tax Event)' only for Head Office; - Ex-date hard block; - Maker cannot approve own; - PII masked in UI; audit immutable.
Audit: TRANSFER_CREATE; VALIDATION_FAIL(ex-date)/PASS; AUTH_ASSIGN; AUTH_DECISION; FILE15_ENQUEUED; 1051_MATCH; 1052_SETTLED; TAX_LOTS_TRANSFERRED.
prerequisites: Customer has TASE equity with available settled qty; pending CA ex-date simulated for one symbol; Head Office and Branch roles available; file gateways active.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Security Transfer -> Select Security: pick two symbols: A (no CA), B (ex-date today) | A selectable, B shows CA indicator | CA indicator shown (Doc A: 12.1) |
| 2 | Call Center | Set Transfer Type=Outside Bank | Allowed for TASE only | If any non-TASE selected earlier -> UI error (Doc A validation #27) |
| 3 | Call Center | For symbol B try to proceed | Hard error per validation #9 | VALIDATION_FAIL(code=EX_DATE_BLOCK) |
| 4 | Call Center | For symbol A enter beneficiary bank code 1245, branch 5124, account 4*******154; Same Entity unchecked; choose Transfer Case 'Transfer between relatives' and subcase 'Sister/brother' | Beneficiary details accepted | Input validation logs; bank/branch lookups |
| 5 | Call Center | Preview, agree commission, Release | Confirmation shows internal transfer ref | TRANSFER_CREATE; commission calc logged |
| 6 | Call Center | Initiate Authorization -> Assign to Pool | Request enqueued | AUTH_ASSIGN pool |
| 7 | Backend | Checker1 picks from Pool, approves | Status U–Authorized; Pend Settle/Open Delivery created | AUTH_DECISION APPROVE; open delivery record |
| 8 | Backend | BOD batch sends File 15 to TASE | File queued successfully | FILE15_ENQUEUED; file path /drop/tase/15/out (ASSUMPTION) |
| 9 | Backend | Receive 1051 recon; mark matched | Reconciliation status=Reconciled | 1051 file ingested; match fields log |
| 10 | Backend | Receive 1052 settlement; settle OD | Transfer settled; custody debited; receiver street settled | Settlement advice linked; movements; tax lot carryover |
| 11 | Call Center | View Security Transfer History | Status Settled; same entity flag value | History row with masked account
expectedResult: Outside bank transfer allowed only for TASE; ex-date block enforced; maker–checker approval flow; settlement completed via 1051/1052; audit trails and lot transfers recorded.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Enter invalid bank/branch code -> lookup error; 2) Agent at Branch selects 'Transfer without Identification (Tax Event)' -> option disabled per role.

title: Security Transfer Within Bank with Virtual Sell and Tax Lots
description: Validate within-bank transfer including Virtual Sell where source=target; EOD auto-settlement, tax engine updates, and audit.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 12.2 Virtual Sell rules; 12.5 Authorization; Doc B: 4.1.1 Within Bank and Virtual Sell; 6 Tax Engine. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent (Maker), Checker.
Security Assertions: - Virtual Sell checkbox only for Within Bank; - Target account auto equals source; - Same-entity computed automatically; - EOD settlement auto; - Tax engine indicators set.
Audit: TRANSFER_CREATE; VIRTUAL_SELL=true; AUTH_DECISION; EOD_SETTLED; TAX_UPDATE_SENT.
prerequisites: Customer has two portfolios; for Virtual Sell, same portfolio used; checker available; EOD job window configured.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Start Security Transfer; choose Within Bank; tick Virtual Sell | Beneficiary account auto-set equals source; transfer case disabled | UI behavior per Doc A: 12.2 |
| 2 | Call Center | Select one security and partial fractional qty (e.g., 12.345) | Fraction accepted | Input precision logged |
| 3 | Call Center | Preview and Release | Confirmation shown | TRANSFER_CREATE with flags |
| 4 | Call Center | Assign to specific Checker | Assigned | AUTH_ASSIGN logged |
| 5 | Backend | Checker approves | Authorized | AUTH_DECISION=APPROVE |
| 6 | Backend | EOD process posts movements | Status Settled; positions moved logically target=source (virtual) | Movement journal with same portfolio both sides; indicator set |
| 7 | Backend | Send lots update to Tax Engine | Tax lots flagged per virtual sell | File/message to tax engine; TAX_UPDATE_SENT |
| 8 | Call Center | History shows Settled and Same Entity true | UI displays flags | History table updated
expectedResult: Virtual Sell available only in Within Bank flow; target auto set to source; authorized and settled at EOD; tax engine receives correct indicators; audit complete.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Try Virtual Sell with Outside Bank -> control disabled; 2) Attempt to edit beneficiary account -> disabled under Virtual Sell.

title: PII Masking in UI, Exports, and Logs
description: Verify PII masking rules across screens, exports, and server logs.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: masking examples across screens (Figures show masked account/portfolio); 2.1/2.2; 3.5/9 exports. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Auditor.
Security Assertions: - Portfolio/account refs masked in UI lists; - PAN 4111********1111 masked if present; - No full National ID or phone displayed; - Logs contain tokenized IDs.
prerequisites: Customer has phone +972*****1234 and email a***@domain.com; test PAN placeholder in notes (ASSUMPTION).
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Authenticate and open Portfolio Valuation | Account number masked e.g., XXX-XX1456 | UI snapshot diff |
| 2 | Call Center | Open Order Book, Trade Book, Reports | Refs masked consistently | Exports preview show masked values |
| 3 | Call Center | Export Order History to Excel | Export contains masked refs; no National ID | Open CSV; regex scan for 9+ digit IDs |
| 4 | Backend | Search logs around actions | No raw phone/email/PAN | Log PII scanner results |
| 5 | Call Center | Enter PAN 4111********1111 in comments (if field exists) | Stored masked; display masked | DB column stores masked/tokenized (ASSUMPTION) |
| 6 | Call Center | View Admin Messages/News | No PII intermix | Content scan |
| 7 | Backend | Check audit events carry internal ids only | Pseudonymous ids; mapping secured | Access-controlled table mapping |
| 8 | Call Center | Attempt to copy full account from DOM | Clipboard has masked value | Browser-level mask (ASSUMPTION)
expectedResult: PII masked consistently across UI and exports; logs/audits contain no raw PII; masking enforced on inputs and outputs.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Attempt URL tampering to fetch full account -> server rejects/returns masked; 2) Export via API directly -> same masking applied.

title: Session Timeout, Logout Invalidation, and Step-up for Sensitive Actions
description: Validate idle timeout, global logout, and step-up re-auth before releasing sensitive transactions.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 12.4/12.5 Release/Authorization suggests sensitive operations; 2.1 SSO. ASSUMPTION: Idle timeout 15 min; step-up re-auth for Release.
Security Assertions: - Idle sessions terminated; - Logout invalidates tokens; - Re-auth required before 'Release' security transfer.
Audit: SESSION_TIMEOUT; LOGOUT; STEP_UP_REQUIRED; STEP_UP_SUCCESS.
prerequisites: Active session; pending Security Transfer on Preview step.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Idle for configured period | Session expires; redirect to login on next action | SESSION_TIMEOUT audit |
| 2 | Call Center | Login again; navigate back to pending transfer draft | Draft restored securely | Draft tied to user; no exposure |
| 3 | Call Center | Click Release on Security Transfer | Step-up re-auth (password/OTP) prompt (ASSUMPTION) | STEP_UP_REQUIRED logged |
| 4 | Call Center | Provide correct step-up | Release proceeds | STEP_UP_SUCCESS |
| 5 | Call Center | Logout | Returned to login; tokens invalidated | LOGOUT audit |
| 6 | Call Center | Reuse old URL or open tab | Redirect to login; 401 APIs | No active session record |
| 7 | Backend | Verify single-session policy if enabled | Any previous sessions terminated | Session store shows only latest |
| 8 | Backend | Ensure step-up window short-lived | Can’t reuse step-up token | Token expiry in logs
expectedResult: Idle timeout and logout properly invalidate sessions; sensitive 'Release' requires step-up; audit reflects all transitions.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Try to submit Release via stale CSRF/session -> rejected; 2) Attempt to reuse step-up token -> denied.

title: RBAC: Prevent Maker from Self-Approving and Enforce Pool Rules
description: Ensure makers cannot approve their own transactions and pool queues work as designed with full audit.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 13 Authorization; 12.5 Security Transfer Authorization; Figures 70–79. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent (Maker), Checker1/Checker2, Auditor.
Security Assertions: - UI prevents self-selection; - Backend enforces actor separation; - Pool items can be picked by any checker but logs who took it.
Audit: AUTH_ASSIGN; AUTH_PICKED_FROM_POOL; AUTH_DECISION; POLICY_VIOLATION if attempted self-approve.
prerequisites: Create a pending approval (order or transfer) from Agent A; Checker roles available.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | As Agent A, generate approval-required order | Approval popup appears | EXCEPTION_RAISED |
| 2 | Call Center | Try Assign to Specific -> choose self | UI validation error 'Self-approval not permitted' | No AUTH_ASSIGN emitted |
| 3 | Call Center | Assign to Pool | Success | AUTH_ASSIGN pool logged |
| 4 | Backend | Checker B opens Pool Queue and picks item | Item assigned to Checker B | AUTH_PICKED_FROM_POOL with userId B |
| 5 | Backend | Checker B approves | Authorized | AUTH_DECISION APPROVE |
| 6 | Backend | Attempt API call as Agent A to approve via direct endpoint (abuse) | 403 Forbidden; event logged | POLICY_VIOLATION logged |
| 7 | Call Center | E-Journal tabs show Initiated By Me/Authorized By Me correctly | Visibility correct | Journal entries present |
| 8 | Auditor | Review audit trail | Complete and immutable | Audit integrity checks
expectedResult: RBAC separation guaranteed; maker cannot self-approve; pool queue operations auditable end-to-end.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Checker tries to delete audit via UI -> not possible; 2) Two checkers race to pick from pool -> only one succeeds (optimistic lock).

title: Foreign Trade with Average Price Re-capture and SEC/TAF Fees
description: Validate EOD foreign average price re-capture, proper fees (SEC/TAF) handling, and reconciliation with broker files.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 3.2 Foreign Trade Settlement Lifecycle; Broker file; fees mapping; SEC/TAF handling; settlement batches. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Back Office operator.
Security Assertions: - Fees marked as 3rd party; - Average price re-capture replaces intraday trades; - Idempotent processing; - No fee leakage in logs.
Audit: FOREIGN_ORDER; BROKER_FILE_INGEST; DEAL_CANCELLED; AVG_PRICE_DEAL_CREATED; FEE_APPLIED_SEC/TAF; STREET_SETTLEMENT_POSTED.
prerequisites: Foreign equity order executed intraday; broker OrderSummary file available at EOD; 872 files available for later recon.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Place foreign Buy; confirm | Order placed | FOREIGN_ORDER |
| 2 | Backend | During day, capture trades | Preliminary executions exist | Deals recorded |
| 3 | Backend | EOD ingest Broker OrderSummary | System cancels intraday deals; creates single avg price deal | BROKER_FILE_INGEST; DEAL_CANCELLED xN; AVG_PRICE_DEAL_CREATED |
| 4 | Backend | Apply fees SEC (sell-only policy noted), TAF (sell-only) where applicable (if sell scenario used) | Fees tagged as 3rd party per Doc B | FEE_APPLIED_SEC/TAF = true with amounts |
| 5 | Backend | Settlement batch on T+1 marks customer side settled | Movements posted | Customer settlement batch logs |
| 6 | Backend | Street side settlement batch marks OD settled | Open deliveries settled | Street settlement batch logs |
| 7 | Backend | Provide recon extract for 872 | Data generated | Extract file present |
| 8 | Call Center | Trade Book reflects avg price entry | Single trade entry shown | UI vs backend consistency
expectedResult: Foreign trade re-captured at average price; correct fee application; settlements posted; reconciliation artifacts available; audits complete.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Duplicate broker file -> processed idempotently; 2) Fees missing in file -> alert and default safe handling without overcharge.

title: Reconciliation of TASE Files 32/1091 and Exception Handling
description: Validate ingestion of net settlement (32) and fee details (1091), reconciliation and mismatch reporting.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 3.1 Net Settlement with files 32/1091; recon options. Systems: Backend primarily; Call Center for reports view. Roles: BO operator, Auditor.
Security Assertions: - Files stored as-is; - Recon depends on setup; - Mismatches reported without exposing PII; - No manual tampering accepted without audit.
Audit: FILE32_INGEST; FILE1091_INGEST; RECON_RUN; RECON_MISMATCH with keys; REPORT_GENERATED.
prerequisites: Trades executed same day; TASE files 32 and 1091 available; commission setup per bank option.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Backend | Drop 32 file to inbound | File ingested and stored | FILE32_INGEST logged with checksum |
| 2 | Backend | Drop 1091 file | Ingested | FILE1091_INGEST |
| 3 | Backend | Run recon job | Mismatches identified (if any) | RECON_RUN with counts |
| 4 | Backend | Generate recon report | CSV produced to vault | REPORT_GENERATED path |
| 5 | Call Center | If report exposed, authorized users can view summary | No PII; masked ids | UI check |
| 6 | Backend | Adjust if TASE fee setup available | Recompute comparisons | Logs show applied setup |
| 7 | Auditor | Verify integrity of stored files | Checksums match; retention OK | Storage scan |
| 8 | Backend | Try to re-upload same file | Duplicate detection | Idempotent processing logs
expectedResult: Files ingested; recon executed; mismatches reported; secure storage and audit of all operations.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Upload malformed file -> rejected with clear technical error; 2) Attempt to edit stored file -> blocked and audited.

title: Abuse: OTP Replay and Session Hijack Attempt during Authorization
description: Attempt to reuse OTP and hijack session during sensitive authorization flow; system must resist.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 12.5 Authorization; 2.1 SSO. ASSUMPTION: OTP is one-time, bound to session; CSRF tokens per form.
Security Assertions: - OTP replay blocked; - Session binding enforced; - CSRF validated; - Audit of suspicious activity.
prerequisites: Pending transaction requires step-up; OTP issued; two browsers (A legitimate, B attacker) simulate hijack.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center (Browser A) | Trigger step-up for Release; receive OTP | OTP displayed to tester (simulator) | MFA_CHALLENGE issued |
| 2 | Call Center (Browser A) | Use OTP to proceed | Success | MFA_VERIFY_SUCCESS |
| 3 | Call Center (Browser B) | Try to reuse same OTP | Rejected 'Expired/Used' | MFA_VERIFY_FAIL reason=REPLAY |
| 4 | Attacker | Copy session cookie from A to B (simulated) | Server detects token mismatch/device binding and forces re-auth | Audit SESSION_ANOMALY; tokens invalidated |
| 5 | Call Center | Attempt POST without CSRF | 403 Forbidden | CSRF_FAIL logged |
| 6 | Backend | Ensure rate limit on OTP | Excess attempts throttled | Rate-limit logs |
| 7 | Call Center | Complete authorization with fresh token | Success | AUTH_DECISION logged |
| 8 | Auditor | Review security alerts | Alerts visible | Security events feed
expectedResult: OTP replay/hijack attempts fail; tokens invalidated; clear security audit trail; legitimate flow succeeds with fresh authentication.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Try to guess OTP length via error -> constant message; 2) Try step-up bypass via direct API -> 401.

title: Abuse: Enumeration via Customer Authentication and Error Messaging
description: Attempt to enumerate accounts/IDs via error responses and timing; system must provide uniform, masked errors.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 2.1 Customer Authentication UX. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent (attacker mindset), Auditor.
Security Assertions: - Uniform error for valid/invalid IDs; - No timing side-channel; - Rate limiting.
Audit: ENUMERATION_SUSPECT (if threshold crossed).
prerequisites: List of random IDs; network timing tool.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
| 1 | Call Center | Submit 20 random National IDs rapidly | All show same generic error | Response time within steady band |
| 2 | Backend | Observe rate limiting after threshold (ASSUMPTION) | Temporary block | Logs show RATE_LIMIT trig |
| 3 | Call Center | Submit one real ID | Same flow with OTP (if enabled) | Customer verify success only after OTP |
| 4 | Backend | Compare latency curves | No distinguishable delta >100ms (ASSUMPTION) | Timing metrics |
| 5 | Auditor | Check alerts | ENUMERATION_SUSPECT event | Security dashboard
expectedResult: No enumeration possible via error content/timing; rate limit applies; alerts generated.
prerequisites_additional: Negative/Abuse: 1) Script via API -> same protections; 2) Language/locale switching does not change error semantics.

title: Tax Simulation to Sell and End-to-End Tax Posting
description: Validate Tax Simulation leading to a sell order and tax postings on settlement with full audit and masking.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 4.2 Tax Simulation; 3.2/3.5/3.6 Order/Trade/Books; 5.1 Fund Balance. Doc B: 6 Tax Engine; 3.1 Local trade settlement; 5 Cash/Security Movements. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent (Maker), Back Office, Auditor. Security goals: step-up before placing sell (ASSUMPTION), PII masking, immutable audit.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Enter quantity greater than available -> secure validation; 2) Modify price after simulation to attempt tax mismatch -> backend recomputes and posts correct tax on settlement.
Automation: High (UI + API + DB/log parsers; Playwright + REST; file watchers for 1051/1052; Kafka/log).
prerequisites: Customer portfolio 04-131-2****7 with TASE equity '519017' and available settled qty; Tax Engine integration active; file gateways for 1051/1052 available; step-up OTP simulator (ASSUMPTION).
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Call Center | Authenticate customer (+ step-up if required). Open Account & Portfolio -> Tax Simulation (Doc A: 4.2). | Tax Simulation grid displays holdings; masked account refs. | UI shows masked refs (XXX-XX1456). Audit CUSTOMER_VERIFY, SCREEN_OPEN.
 2 | Call Center | Select symbol 519017, set Quantity=10, Price=1,793 (ILS). Click Simulate. | Simulation result shows Profit/Loss, Taxable Amount, Tax to Pay, and 'Sell' link (Fig. 34). | Audit TAX_SIMULATE with inputs/outputs; no PII in logs.
 3 | Call Center | Click Sell from simulation line. | Equity order entry opens prefilled (Doc A: 3.2). | Audit ORDER_CREATE_INTENT linking TAX_SIM_REF.
 4 | Call Center | Preview -> Confirm order. | Order placed; internal order number displayed; commission shown. | ORDER_CREATE; COMMISSION_PREVIEW records.
 5 | Backend | Perform pre-trade checks and authorize (Doc B: 2 Validations). | Order status Authorized/Placed With Market. | ORDER_AUTHORIZED; risk check logs.
 6 | Backend | Ingest 1051 on T; process executions. | Deal(s) captured; recon status=Reconciled. | 1051_INGEST; DEAL_RECON=Reconciled.
 7 | Backend | Ingest 1052 on T+1; settle. Trigger Tax Engine EOD postings (Doc B: 6). | Cash/security movements posted; tax/refund movement posted to customer account. | MOVEMENTS(CR/DR) with BSPL, value date; TAX_POSTING with lot closures.
 8 | Call Center | View Trade Book/Order History and Fund Balance. | Executed trade visible; Fund Balance shows tax debit/credit lines; masking intact. | UI vs DB amounts; tax movement narration present.
 9 | Auditor | Review audit trail. | Immutable chain: TAX_SIMULATE -> ORDER_CREATE -> EXECUTE -> TAX_POST. | Audit store contains user/role/BP, symbol, qty, before/after balances, IP, timestamps.
expectedResult: Tax Simulation correctly computes preview; Sell order executes and settles; tax postings created on settlement; all UI shows masked identifiers; full audit trail present and consistent.

title: Corporate Action Option Capture by Call Center with Step-up and Audit
description: Verify that call center can record CA options on behalf of customer, with proper step-up, RBAC, masking, and audit.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 10 Corporate Action (CA icon flow from Portfolio Valuation and Detailed Portfolio Valuation Figs. 37–40); 2.1/2.2 masking; 13 Authorization (for any approvals if required). Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Supervisor (if approval required), Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Attempt CA option for a portfolio not in session -> blocked; 2) Attempt to change CA option after cut-off -> secure error without leaking issuer details.
Automation: Medium (UI flow + audit/log checks).
prerequisites: Portfolio holds a security with an active CA requiring option selection (CA icon visible per Doc A: 40). Step-up challenge enabled for sensitive updates (ASSUMPTION).
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Call Center | Authenticate, open Detailed Portfolio Valuation; locate security with 'C' CA indicator. | CA icon visible; PII masked. | UI snapshot; masking verified.
 2 | Call Center | Click CA icon -> Choose Option. | CA option dialog opens with options and required inputs. | Audit CA_VIEW with BP ID (tokenized), portfolio.
 3 | Call Center | Initiate option submission; system prompts for step-up (ASSUMPTION). | Step-up modal shown. | STEP_UP_REQUIRED audit.
 4 | Call Center | Enter valid OTP/password; submit option. | Success confirmation; reference id generated. | CA_OPTION_SUBMIT with option id, before/after state, ip/terminal.
 5 | Backend | Validate RBAC and persist CA instruction; link to portfolio and event id. | State change recorded; no immediate market interaction. | DB CA tables updated; immutable audit written.
 6 | Call Center | View 'View Response' list for the security. | New response present with timestamp and masked account id. | UI list vs DB.
 7 | Backend | If maker–checker policy enabled for CA (bank policy), Supervisor approves. | Approved; status moves to Authorized. | AUTH_DECISION(APPROVE) for CA.
 8 | Auditor | Review end-to-end audit. | Complete chain with user/role, timestamps, IP, terminal id; no PII in error logs. | Audit integrity checks.
expectedResult: CA option recorded with step-up auth; masking holds; optional maker–checker enforced; authoritative audit and state saved in backend.

title: Mutual Fund Order Parameters and Cut-off Handling (Files 126/544)
description: Ensure MF orders respect trading parameters and cut-off; allocations/settlements processed with correct statuses.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 3.3 MF Purchase/Redeem, NAV List; Doc B: 3.6 MF trading (file 126 parameters, 544 cut-offs, 1051/1052 flows). Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Back Office, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Place MF order after cut-off expecting same-day -> system queues to next cycle with info banner; 2) Enter negative/zero units -> validation error with no sensitive data.
Automation: High (UI + file watchers for 1051/1052 + logs).
prerequisites: MF product configured; latest files 126 and 544 loaded; customer has sufficient funds; NAV List visible.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Call Center | From NAV List select scheme 'HRL SKL WITHOUT', click Purchase at T-5 minutes before cut-off (per file 544). | Create screen prefilled (Doc A: Fig. 19). | ENTITLEMENT check; PARAM_SOURCE=file544.
 2 | Call Center | Enter Units=100; Place Order -> Preview -> Confirm. | Confirmation with internal order id; status 'Order Placed'. | MF_ORDER_CREATE; commission breakdown.
 3 | Backend | Validate against file 126 parameters (min amount, increments). | Order accepted/flagged warnings per params. | PARAM_VALIDATION_PASS; warnings logged.
 4 | Backend | Ingest 1051 allocation (T evening). | Allocation captured; units allocated. | 1051_ALLOC; MF_ALLOCATION event.
 5 | Backend | Process 1052 street settlement (T+1 20:30). | Settlement posted; positions updated. | MOVEMENTS customer/street; OD settled.
 6 | Call Center | Check MF Order Book and Order Trail. | Allocation and settlement events visible; masked account. | UI vs DB alignment.
 7 | Call Center | Attempt another Purchase after cut-off (T+1 past cut-off). | UI warning: will be processed next business cycle; proceed to queue. | CUT_OFF_INFO; order value date set next cycle.
 8 | Auditor | Verify audit events and that no PII appears in messages/logs. | Complete immutable record. | Audit store scan.
expectedResult: MF order adheres to cut-off/parameters; allocations and settlements recorded through 1051/1052; UI and backend consistent; audit complete and secure.

title: Extranet Trade Capture and Reconciliation
description: Validate off-floor (Extranet) trade handling, pre-placed blocks, BO manual capture and settlement.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 3.3 Extranet Trades; 3.1 TASE lifecycle; 5 Movements. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Back Office operator, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Use wrong OrderCategory when capturing BO trade -> rejection with secure error; 2) Forget to expire pre-placed blocks -> reconciliation report highlights inconsistency.
Automation: Medium (UI + BO screens + file watchers + report parsing).
prerequisites: Portfolio with funds/securities; Extranet desk will execute a trade; 1051/1052 files available next days.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Call Center | Receive off-floor request; place cash/custody block per side (buy/sell). | Blocks visible in Fund Balance -> Blocked cash details. | BLOCK_CREATE entries with internal refs.
 2 | External | Execute trade via Extranet platform (out of scope UI). | Execution occurs. | NA.
 3 | Backend | Ingest 1051 on T. | Mismatch report shows extranet trade not in BaNCS orders. | RSP34290 (or recon) shows exception line.
 4 | Back Office | Manually capture trade next morning (T+1) marking OrderCategory='Extranet transaction out of exchange'. | Trade created; linked to portfolio; blocks expired. | BO screen entries; MOVEMENTS created; block release audit.
 5 | Backend | Process 1052 settlement. | Settlement posted; open deliveries matched. | Open delivery -> Settled; settlement advice linked.
 6 | Call Center | Trade History reflects captured trade; status Executed. | UI list shows masked account; correct price/qty. | Compare with BO data.
 7 | Auditor | Validate audit chain and that manual capture used correct category. | AUDIT: MANUAL_TRADE_CAPTURE with user/role; reason=Extranet. | Audit immutability check.
 8 | Backend | Run recon again. | Exception resolved. | Recon status cleared.
expectedResult: Extranet trade processed securely: pre-placed blocks, BO capture with correct category, settlement and recon clear; audit complete.

title: Order Amendments Post-Execution with Four-Eyes Control
description: Verify BO order amendments (cancel/change/missing) require approvals and produce correct reversals and accounting.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 3.4 Order Amendments 3.4.1/3.4.2/3.4.3; 5 Cash/Security Movement creation; Limits: Correction between correction account and client requires 4-eyes approval (Doc B: 2 Blocks). Systems: Backend primarily; Call Center for visibility. Roles: BO Maker, BO Checker, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) BO Maker attempts amendment without checker approval -> blocked; 2) Attempt to edit accounting postings directly -> forbidden; must go via amendment workflow.
Automation: Medium (BO UI + DB movement checks + audit).
prerequisites: An executed order exists (internal order id e.g., 20200903******). BO roles and 4-eyes policy configured.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Backend | Open Order Amendment screen (Doc B screenshots). Choose 'ExistingOrder (Change or Correction)'. | Form opens with order id. | AUDIT: AMEND_INTENT.
 2 | Backend | Select fields to modify (e.g., Price). Provide Correction Portfolio if required. | Amendment draft created. | Draft state stored; no postings yet.
 3 | Backend | Submit amendment -> routes for approval. | Status 'Pending Authorization'. | AUTH_ASSIGN to Checker; E-Journal updated.
 4 | Backend (Checker) | Approve amendment. | Authorization recorded; amendment applied. | AUTH_DECISION APPROVE; AMEND_APPLIED.
 5 | Backend | System posts reversal of original movement(s) and new corrected movement(s). | Double-entry integrity; net reflects corrected trade. | MOVEMENT pairs with references; BSPL and value dates correct.
 6 | Call Center | Order/Trade History reflect corrected details. | UI shows updated price/values; mask intact. | Compare pre/post snapshots.
 7 | Backend | Try 'Cancel Existing Order' path for different executed order. | Reversal posted; order marked cancelled; linked audit. | CXL_REVERSAL postings; audit chain.
 8 | Backend | Use 'Missing Order' to capture a missed executed order (if present in recon). | Order captured; approvals applied. | MISSING_ORDER_CAPTURE; approvals/audit.
expectedResult: BO amendments enforce 4-eyes; corrections generate reversal/replacement movements; Call Center reflects updates; audit trail complete and immutable.

title: Fractional Sell Leftover Settlement to Bank Fraction Portfolio
description: Validate batch handling of fractional leftovers from full sells and settlement to bank fraction portfolio.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 3.5 Fractional Order; 3.1/5 Movements. Doc A note: Full Sell fractional not supported in UI (POTENTIALLY STALE vs BO batch) – ASSUMPTION: fractional leftovers may occur and are handled by BO batch. Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Back Office, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Duplicate batch run -> idempotent; 2) Fraction value rounding errors -> tolerance rules applied.
Automation: Medium (batch verification + DB movement checks).
prerequisites: Customer holds quantity with fraction when selling (e.g., 10.75 units). Bank fraction portfolio configured.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Call Center | Place Sell order intending to liquidate position resulting in fractional leftover (ASSUMPTION: via partial sells). | Order placed/executed. | ORDER_CREATE/EXECUTE.
 2 | Backend | EOD batch scans for fractional leftovers (Doc B: 3.5). | Batch identifies fractional remainder. | BATCH_FRACTION_SCAN log.
 3 | Backend | Batch creates 'Transit Deal' between client and fraction portfolio at average price of the day. | Deal posted; OD created. | DEAL_CREATE with category=Transit; price=avg of deals.
 4 | Backend | On 1052 processing, settle client vs fraction portfolio movement. | Movements posted; OD settled. | MOVEMENT settlement entries; OD status=Settled.
 5 | Call Center | Portfolio Activity reflects fractional adjustment. | Movement description indicates fraction settlement. | UI vs DB line check.
 6 | Auditor | Review audit and confirm no PII leaked. | Events: FRACTION_DEAL_CREATE; FRACTION_SETTLE. | Audit integrity checks.
 7 | Backend | Re-run batch (idempotence test). | No duplicate deals created. | BATCH idempotence log; no extra postings.
 8 | Backend | Verify rounding applied per currency minor units. | Rounding consistent with major/minor factors. | Movement amounts precise.
expectedResult: Fractional leftovers settled automatically to bank fraction portfolio with accurate pricing, idempotent behavior, and complete audit.

title: Incoming Security Transfers via File 132 with Repair Workflow
description: Validate processing of incoming transfers, including To-Be-Repaired status and subsequent repair/settlement.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 4 Stock Transfers (Incoming 132), validations, lots carryover; 4.1 types; recon with 1051/1052. Doc A: 12.1 CA indicator/ex-date block (for visibility only). Systems: Backend primarily; Call Center for history view. Roles: BO Operator, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Beneficiary account not found -> To Be Repaired; 2) Layer quantities mismatch -> repair required; secure error messages.
Automation: Medium (file ingestion watchers + BO screens).
prerequisites: File drop location accessible; sample 132 with two records: one valid, one with wrong beneficiary id.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Backend | Drop 132 file with two records. ASSUMPTION: poll every 30s up to 10m. | File ingested; Delivery In created. | FILE132_INGEST log with checksum.
 2 | Backend | System validates technical/business fields. | Record A -> Authorized; Record B -> To Be Repaired. | STATUS per record; validation logs for mismatch.
 3 | Back Office | Open Incoming Transfer list; filter 'To Be Repaired'. | See Record B; reason shown. | UI rows; reason codes.
 4 | Back Office | Repair beneficiary mapping/lot layers; save. | Status moves to Authorized. | REPAIR_APPLIED audit.
 5 | Backend | Ingest 1051/1052 for settlement. | Open Delivery -> Settled; lots carried over. | OPEN_DELIVERY status flow; lot table updated.
 6 | Call Center | View Security Transfer History for customer. | Transfer In entry with status Settled; masked refs. | UI vs DB.
 7 | Auditor | Review audit events. | FILE_INGEST, VALIDATION_FAIL, REPAIR, AUTHORIZED, SETTLED with keys (portfolio, symbol, qty, same-entity flag). | Audit store check.
 8 | Backend | Ensure option to cancel at BO if bank blocks transfer. | Cancelled state recorded; no settlement. | CANCEL event and no movements.
expectedResult: File 132 transfers processed; invalid records moved to repair and then authorized; settlements posted; lots preserved; audit comprehensive.

title: Third-Party Custodian Delivery In/Out (Not Sent to TASE)
description: Verify Delivery In/Out for third-party custodian transfers, ensuring no TASE file generated and reports produced.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 4.1.3 Delivery In/Out and Third Party Transfer; 4 Stock Transfers notes; Reports list (RSP34100/34101 etc.). Systems: Backend primarily; Call Center for view-only. Roles: BO Operator, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Mistakenly mark as Outside Bank -> should not enqueue File 15; 2) Attempt to post without required external references -> validation error.
Automation: Medium (BO UI + file-outbox watchers + report parsers).
prerequisites: BO access; external custodian relationship; reporting enabled.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Backend | Create Delivery Out for foreign security; select transfer case 'Third party Transfer'. | Order created. | TRANSFER_CREATE with case=ThirdParty.
 2 | Backend | EOD/BOD cycles. | No File 15 generated for this transfer. | Outbox /drop/tase/15/out remains without this item; FILE15_ENQUEUED absent.
 3 | Backend | Generate report for third-party transfer details. | Report produced to secure vault. | Jasper/CSV presence; row includes masked identifiers.
 4 | Backend | Capture incoming Delivery In upon custodian confirmation. | Positions updated; audit recorded. | MOVEMENTS; DELIVERY_IN_AUTH.
 5 | Call Center | Security Transfer History shows Third Party transfer entries. | Visible with status and masked account. | UI vs DB.
 6 | Auditor | Verify audit events and no leakage to TASE interfaces. | Audit: DELIVERY_OUT_THIRDPARTY; DELIVERY_IN_THIRDPARTY. | Interface logs show no File 15/1051 dependency.
 7 | Backend | Attempt to incorrectly mark 'Outside Bank' on same transfer. | Validation blocks (should be Third Party flow only). | VALIDATION_FAIL with secure message.
 8 | Backend | Ensure manual settlement option recorded if required. | Manual settle logged with approver. | MANUAL_SETTLE audit event.
expectedResult: Third-party Delivery In/Out stays off TASE interfaces; reports generated; settlements recorded; audits complete and secure.

title: In-job Activities Access Governance and No-Auth Trading Prevention
description: Ensure In-job Activities expose historical/aggregate views but prevent customer-specific actions without authentication.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 8 In-job Activities (Order/Trade/MF History, Security Holding) – no customer auth required; 2 Understanding the Interface; 2.1/2.2. Systems: Both (auth checks in backend). Roles: Agent (branch), Head Office user, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Attempt to place order from In-job screen via hidden endpoint -> 401/403; 2) Head Office user attempts to export PII outside permitted fields -> redaction.
Automation: High (UI + API negative tests + logs).
prerequisites: Agent logged in without selecting a customer; In-job data seeded; HO user exists.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Call Center | Navigate to In-job Order Book (Doc A: 8.1). | Data visible for branch; no customer auth needed. | ENTITLEMENT_DECISION=ALLOW_VIEW only.
 2 | Call Center | Attempt to open Order Entry from In-job context. | Redirect to Customer Authentication; cannot proceed. | BACKEND 401 for trade APIs; masked error.
 3 | Call Center | Open Security Holding (Doc A: 8.3) -> filter; export. | Export contains permitted columns only; PII masked. | Export scanner finds no full IDs.
 4 | Call Center (HO user) | Repeat views selecting ALL call centers. | HO sees all; branch-restricted in other role. | RBAC_DECISION logs.
 5 | Backend | Try API call to place order with no customer context. | 403 Forbidden. | SECURITY_EVENT: MISSING_CUSTOMER_CONTEXT.
 6 | Call Center | View In-job MF History; drill to internal order number. | Drill-down read-only; no modify/cancel. | UI controls disabled; audit VIEW_ONLY.
 7 | Auditor | Review access logs. | In-job views audited with user/role/call center id; no writes. | Audit integrity.
 8 | Backend | Pen-test export endpoints for PII leakage. | Masking applied consistently. | Log PII linter clean.
expectedResult: In-job modules allow read-only, masked views; trading actions require customer authentication; RBAC and audit enforced.

title: Authorization E-Journal Integrity and Access Control
description: Validate that E-Journal shows Initiated/Authorized items, cannot be altered/deleted, and is accessible by Auditor role.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 13 Authorization, E-Journal (Fig. 77–78). Systems: Both. Roles: Agent, Checker, Auditor. Security goals: immutability, RBAC segregation.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Attempt to delete E-Journal entry -> denied; 2) Non-auditor tries to access other users’ E-Journal -> RBAC denial.
Automation: Medium (UI + API negative + DB immutability check).
prerequisites: At least one order/transfer processed with maker–checker to populate E-Journal.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Call Center | Open Authorization -> E-Journal -> Initiated By Me. | List shows items initiated by user. | UI list; audit for VIEW.
 2 | Call Center | Switch to 'Authorized By Me'. | Items authorized by user appear. | Correct scoping confirmed.
 3 | Backend | Attempt DELETE/PUT on journal item via API (simulated). | 403 Forbidden; immutable. | SECURITY_EVENT: JOURNAL_TAMPER_BLOCKED.
 4 | Call Center (Agent) | Try to view another user’s E-Journal directly via URL. | Access denied; generic error. | RBAC_DECISION=DENY.
 5 | Call Center (Auditor) | Access consolidated E-Journal view. | Read-only access to all; export allowed with masking. | Auditor role logs; export sanitized.
 6 | Backend | Verify records are write-once with append-only audit. | No updates; only inserts. | DB constraints/trigger logs.
 7 | Auditor | Cross-check a sampled item with underlying order/transfer audit. | References consistent. | Correlation successful.
 8 | Backend | Check retention policy (ASSUMPTION per bank policy). | Records persist per retention; hash/chain if configured. | Retention config.
expectedResult: E-Journal is immutable, properly scoped by role, and correlates to underlying transactions; audit/retention policies enforced.

title: Regulatory Fee/Commission Reporting (BOI 414; TASE 814/815)
description: Verify generation of customer and TASE fee/commission reports with correct aggregation and masking.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 7 Charges/Commission (Reporting to TASE 814/815; BOI Directive 414 A/B/C). Systems: Backend; Call Center optional for customer notice visibility. Roles: BO Operator, Auditor. Security: secure storage, PII masking in exports.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Attempt to modify generated report file -> detect via checksum; 2) Mismatch between computed and reported fee -> discrepancy flagged.
Automation: Medium (batch + file parsing + checksum validation).
prerequisites: Executed trades and MF transactions in the period; reporting jobs configured.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Backend | Run monthly fee aggregation job. | Report artifacts generated (414 A/B/C CSV/PDF to vault). | Job logs; file paths; checksums.
 2 | Backend | Generate TASE 814/815 files. | Files created with schema-compliant records. | Schema validation OK.
 3 | Backend | Validate sample records vs DB charge items (ST Commission, third-party fees). | Amounts reconcile. | SQL recomputation matches file totals.
 4 | Backend | Open Section A (averages) and verify also published to website (ASSUMPTION). | Available via secure channel; no raw PII. | PII scan; access logs.
 5 | Backend | Try to edit a file in place. | Write blocked; or change detected; new checksum. | IMMUTABILITY/Checksum check.
 6 | Call Center | If customer notices visible, view one on behalf of test customer. | Notice shows correct charges; masked identifiers. | UI mask verified.
 7 | Auditor | Review audit events for generation and access. | REPORT_GENERATED, REPORT_ACCESSED with user/role/time. | Audit store check.
 8 | Backend | For 1091 reconciliation dependency (if set up), ensure differences listed. | Recon difference report produced. | Recon logs.
expectedResult: Regulatory reports generated with correct data and masking; files secured and immutable; audit covers generation and access.

title: Position Reconciliation for Local (1053), Foreign (871) and Tax Engine Positions
description: Validate daily EOD position reconciliation across TASE, foreign custodian, and Tax Engine with exception handling and ageing.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc B: 8 Position Recon (8.1 Local 1053; 8.2 Foreign 871; 8.3 Tax Engine recon). Systems: Backend; Call Center only for visibility. Roles: BO Operator, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Introduce a deliberate mismatch -> appears with ageing; 2) Mark an OD as matched manually -> excluded from auto recon.
Automation: High (file/watchers + recon job + reports).
prerequisites: Positions exist; file providers deliver 1053/871; Tax Engine EOD positions available.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Backend | Ingest 1053 (local), 871 (foreign), and Tax Engine position files. | Files accepted; stored with checksums. | FILE_INGEST logs with source tags.
 2 | Backend | Run recon jobs. | Exceptions list generated per source; matched records marked. | RECON_RUN with counts.
 3 | Backend | Open recon module; filter by exception type and source. | Mismatches visible with ageing buckets. | UI vs DB.
 4 | Backend | Manually match a specific OD and settlement advice. | Marked as manually matched; excluded from next run. | MANUAL_MATCH audit; flag set.
 5 | Backend | Fix underlying data for a deliberate mismatch and rerun. | Exception resolved; ageing resets. | RECON_RESOLVED event.
 6 | Auditor | Export exception report. | CSV with masked customer refs. | PII scan passes.
 7 | Backend | Verify Tax Engine vs BaNCS open position totals. | Differences highlighted; to be investigated. | Tax recon report.
 8 | Backend | Ensure retention of input files and recon logs. | Files stored per policy; logs contain no PII. | Storage checks.
expectedResult: Position reconciliations run successfully; mismatches identified and aged; manual matches honored; secure storage and audit maintained.

title: Abuse: Branch User Payload-Tampering to Select 'Transfer without Identification (Tax Event)'
description: Ensure Head Office-only transfer case cannot be exploited by branch via UI tampering or API calls.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 12.2 Security Transfer – Add Beneficiary, Transfer Case, item 21 (Head Office only); item 22 MF restrictions; Doc B: 4 Stock Transfers same-entity identifier mapping. Systems: Both. Roles: Branch Agent (attacker), Head Office user, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Branch tries to set case=6 via modified request -> backend rejects; 2) Attempt MF transfer to different entity -> hard error per item 22.
Automation: High (UI tampering via devtools + direct API calls + log inspection).
prerequisites: Branch Agent role; HO role; portfolio with MF and equity; Security Transfer screen accessible.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Call Center (Branch) | Open Security Transfer; Outside Bank; attempt to pick 'Transfer without Identification (Tax Event)'. | UI: option not available. | UI option list; RBAC_DECISION.
 2 | Call Center (Branch) | Intercept network request and modify payload to case=6. | Backend rejects with 403/validation error; generic message. | POLICY_VIOLATION logged with user/role/ip.
 3 | Call Center (Branch) | Try MF transfer to different entity by selecting MF and unchecking Same Entity. | Error: 'Mutual funds cannot be transferred to other entities' (Doc A item 22). | VALIDATION_FAIL code.
 4 | Call Center (HO) | Perform same selection (case=6) legitimately. | Allowed; info banner about taxation (Doc A item 23). | TRANSFER_CREATE with case=6; Same-Entity Tax Identifier=2.
 5 | Backend | Verify same-entity tax identifier mapping set correctly. | Mapping 2 applied per table. | DB field check.
 6 | Auditor | Review security events. | Clear logs for denied attempts; no PII leaked. | Audit store.
 7 | Backend | Ensure 'Outside Bank' non-TASE block still enforced. | Attempt foreign exchange -> blocked. | VALIDATION_FAIL (Doc A item 27).
 8 | Call Center | Submit allowed HO case; proceed through authorization if required. | Maker–checker operates; transaction continues. | AUTH_ASSIGN/DECISION events.
expectedResult: Branch user cannot select or force tax-event transfer case; HO can; MF different-entity restriction enforced; all attempts audited securely.

title: Security Rates Ingestion and Use of Previous Close for Transfer Valuation
description: Verify that security transfer value uses previous closing price and correct currency handling; rates ingestion secure.
testDescription:
Requirement references: Doc A: 12.1 Add Security (Previous close in trade currency; consolidated value in account currency); 11 Market Data Subscription table; Doc B: 1.2 Security Rates (collect/validate); 1.2.1 Security Rates. Systems: Both. Roles: Back Office (rates operator), Agent, Auditor.
Negative/Abuse variations: 1) Live quote appears in transfer value -> violation; 2) Invalid rate file format -> ingest rejected with secure logs.
Automation: Medium (file ingest + UI checks + DB validation).
prerequisites: Rates feed running or manual upload enabled; portfolio with multi-currency positions.
stepsToPerform:
| Step # | Actor/System | Action | Expected Result | Technical Verification |
 1 | Backend | Upload security rate file for prior day (manual/mass entry). | Rates stored; validation applied. | RATE_INGEST with checksum; validation errors, if any.
 2 | Call Center | Open Security Transfer -> Add Security. | Each row shows Previous Closing Price in trade currency; consolidated Transfer Value in account currency (ILS). | UI currency labels; value math.
 3 | Call Center | Select multiple securities (ILS and USD) and adjust Transfer Quantities (fractions allowed). | Values update; total in account currency. | FX conversion logic applied; rate date=t-1.
 4 | Backend | Confirm value calculations align with rate table. | Accurate multiplication with correct major/minor factors. | SQL recompute vs UI totals.
 5 | Call Center | Ensure no live data populates Previous Close fields. | Banner indicates previous close basis; no live timestamp. | ENTITLEMENT_DECISION shows no live feed used here.
 6 | Backend | Try ingesting malformed rate file. | Rejected with clear technical error; no partial updates. | RATE_INGEST_FAIL; no DB change.
 7 | Auditor | Review audit for rate ingestion/access. | Events present, no PII. | Audit store.
 8 | Call Center | Continue transfer flow (no need to complete). | Values remain stable across refresh. | Deterministic recompute logs.
expectedResult: Transfer valuation uses previous close with correct currency conversions; rate ingestion robust; no live data leakage; audit trails intact.
